feed.title: U.S. District Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
feed.subtitle: The District of Maine's Local Rules and their amendment history — procedural rules governing every civil and criminal case filed in the district. Prose/link page listing rules and linked PDFs with no repeating dated list, monitored by full-page fingerprint so rule amendments surface as change items.

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. District Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + Education Home + Court History + Gignoux Courthouse Accessibility Information Handbook + Judges + Advisory RE: Personal Identifiers + Handout for Self-Represented Parties + Electronic Filing (ECF) + Information for Pro Se Parties + LOCAL RULES + To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software (Adobe 7 or higher). + Local Rules effective December 1, 2022 + Interim Rule 4 – Maine Foreclosure Diversion Pilot + General Order 2021-5 : Interim Procedures for the Filing, Service, and Management of Highly Sensitive Documents + However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us at: + localrules@med.uscourts.gov + . Removed: - Education / Outreach - Accessibility & Accommodations - Judges and Court History - Advisory: Personal Identifiable Information - Information Packet for Self-Represented Parties - Electronic Filing - Legal Support Staff Materials - Case Information by Phone - Court Photos - To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software. - Local Rules effective April 1, 2025 - However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us by submitt [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. District Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + Education / Outreach + Accessibility & Accommodations + Judges and Court History + Advisory: Personal Identifiable Information + Information Packet for Self-Represented Parties + Electronic Filing + Legal Support Staff Materials + Case Information by Phone + Court Photos + To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software. + Local Rules effective April 1, 2025 + However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us by submitting a + contact form here + (select Category: Local Rules) + Pilot Plan to Assign Civil Cases to U.S. Magistrate Judges Removed: - Education Home - Court History - Gignoux Courthouse Accessibility Information Handbook - Judges - Advisory RE: Personal Identifiers - Handout for Self-Represented Parties - Electronic Filing (ECF) - Information for Pro Se Parties - LOCAL RULES - To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software (Adobe 7 or higher). - Local Rules effective December 1, 2022 - Interim Rule 4 – Maine Foreclosure Diversion Pilot - General Order 2021-5 : Interim Procedures for the Filing, Service, and Management of Highly Sensitive Documents - However, we remain open to suggestions and w [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. District Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + Education Home + Court History + Gignoux Courthouse Accessibility Information Handbook + Judges + Advisory RE: Personal Identifiers + Handout for Self-Represented Parties + Electronic Filing (ECF) + Information for Pro Se Parties + LOCAL RULES + To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software (Adobe 7 or higher). + Local Rules effective December 1, 2022 + Interim Rule 4 – Maine Foreclosure Diversion Pilot + General Order 2021-5 : Interim Procedures for the Filing, Service, and Management of Highly Sensitive Documents + However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us at: + localrules@med.uscourts.gov + . Removed: - Education / Outreach - Accessibility & Accommodations - Judges and Court History - Advisory: Personal Identifiable Information - Information Packet for Self-Represented Parties - Electronic Filing - Legal Support Staff Materials - Case Information by Phone - Court Photos - To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software. - Local Rules effective April 1, 2025 - However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us by submitt [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: U.S. District Court, District of Maine — Local Rules
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Added: + Education / Outreach + Accessibility & Accommodations + Judges and Court History + Advisory: Personal Identifiable Information + Information Packet for Self-Represented Parties + Electronic Filing + Legal Support Staff Materials + Case Information by Phone + Court Photos + To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software. + Local Rules effective April 1, 2025 + However, we remain open to suggestions and welcome comments you may have for improving our local rules. You may send them to us by submitting a + contact form here + (select Category: Local Rules) + Pilot Plan to Assign Civil Cases to U.S. Magistrate Judges Removed: - Education Home - Court History - Gignoux Courthouse Accessibility Information Handbook - Judges - Advisory RE: Personal Identifiers - Handout for Self-Represented Parties - Electronic Filing (ECF) - Information for Pro Se Parties - LOCAL RULES - To view individual rules, click on the hyperlinks in the Table of Contents. To search for specific words or phrases, use the Search function in your Adobe software (Adobe 7 or higher). - Local Rules effective December 1, 2022 - Interim Rule 4 – Maine Foreclosure Diversion Pilot - General Order 2021-5 : Interim Procedures for the Filing, Service, and Management of Highly Sensitive Documents - However, we remain open to suggestions and w [diff truncated]
